extraction.one_line_summary: MILAN Yonatan won the sprint on debut after a crash at 3.7km split the field, catching CAVENDISH and dropping GEOGHEGAN HART 6 places on GC.
tactical_patterns: LA PERA attacked both KOM climbs aggressively on debut to secure jersey before home region stage, typical young rider overcommitment.
Break caught at 38km remaining triggered positioning chaos; teams failed to control narrowing roads before 3km rule protection zone.
Crash occurred exactly 700m before 3km banner, suggesting positioning pressure intensifies precisely in that unprotected window before safety rule.

rider_signals.MILAN Yonatan: positive: Won Grand Tour debut sprint showing track power translates to road despite February win drought
rider_signals.CAVENDISH Mark: negative: Caught in late crash despite local knowledge from 2011 win suggests positioning deterioration
rider_signals.GEOGHEGAN HART Tao: negative: Lost 4 GC positions (4th to 8th) on supposed easy stage, revealing weak positioning team support
rider_signals.GROVES Kaden: neutral: Led by SINKELDAM but only 3rd suggests leadout timing issues on debut with team
rider_signals.LA PERA Paul: positive: 22-year-old debutant won KOM jersey through calculated double-climb attack, not break survival
rider_signals.VLASOV Aleksandr: positive: Moved up to 7th GC on flat stage crash carnage, indicating superior positioning instincts
